tool_args.label: MTA Headquarters Procurements
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Headquarters proposes to award Competitive procurements in the following categories: Schedules Requiring Majority Vote: # of Actions $ Amount Schedule F: Personal Service Contracts 2 $40.0M Schedule H: Modifications 1 $23.3M SUBTOTAL $63.3M

payload.title: MTA Headquarters Procurements
payload.description: January 2024 procurement agenda for MTA Headquarters Committee and Board approval covering 3 actions for $63.3M in competitive procurements.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA Headquarters procurement department proposing competitive procurements for January 2024.
Contractor awarded retainer contract for Kronos Systems Integration and Implementation consulting services.
Contractor awarded retainer contract for Kronos Systems Integration and Implementation consulting services.
Vendor awarded contract to implement Contact Center as a Service for Paratransit. Located in Clifton Park, New York.

payload.entity_name: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights
payload.description: Established no MBE, WBE, or SDVOB goals for retainer contracts due to lack of certified firms with required UKG Dimensions experience.

payload.entity_name: New York City Transit Paratransit Department
payload.description: NYCT Paratransit contact center to be added to CCaaS implementation. Located in Long Island City, handles about 9 million calls per year.
UKG (Ultimate Kronos Group) provides workforce management software including UKG Workforce Central (WFC) and UKG Dimensions SaaS platforms.
Provides Genesys Cloud Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) platform. Recognized by Gartner's Magic Quadrant as a leader in CCaaS marketplace.
MTA IT Department manages Kronos Systems Integration and CCaaS projects.

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.description: Two competitively negotiated retainer contracts with IBM and Accenture to provide consulting services for UKG Dimensions timekeeping platform migration from UKG Workforce Central (WFC).

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.project_name: Contact Center as a Service for Paratransit
payload.description: Modification to contract with DiRAD Technologies to expand existing MTA CCaaS implementation to include NYCT Paratransit Contact Center using Genesys Cloud platform.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.description: Estimated aggregate budget for two retainer contracts with IBM and Accenture for Kronos Systems Integration and Implementation.
Estimated value of modification to contract with DiRAD to implement CCaaS for Paratransit Contact Center.
Paratransit Contact Center handles about 9 million calls each year.
Peak staffing of about 670 simultaneously active representatives at Paratransit Contact Center.
About 80 supervisors at Paratransit Contact Center.

payload.raw_value_text: about $1.2 million annually, or about $8.4 million over the contract period
payload.description: Expected annual cost elimination from supporting existing on-premise contact center systems and telecommunications after CCaaS transition.

payload.raw_value_text: about $1.2 million annually, or about $8.4 million over the contract period
payload.description: Expected total cost elimination over the contract period from supporting existing on-premise contact center systems after CCaaS transition.
Contract price of $185 per concurrent CCaaS license per month for DiRAD contract.

payload.raw_value_text: 750 concurrent CCaaS licenses required by Paratransit, and an additional 150 licenses to allow for growth across all MTA Agencies
payload.description: Total of 900 concurrent CCaaS licenses: 750 for Paratransit plus 150 for growth across all MTA Agencies.

payload.raw_value_text: IBM's negotiated hourly labor rates range from $173 to $270
payload.description: IBM's negotiated hourly labor rates range from $173 to $270.

payload.raw_value_text: Accenture's negotiated hourly labor rates range from $99 to $375.50
payload.description: Accenture's negotiated hourly labor rates range from $99 to $375.50.
MTA Headquarters Committee meeting to consider procurement actions.
MTA Board meeting to consider procurement actions.

payload.event_name: RFP Issued for Kronos Systems Integration
payload.description: A Request for Proposals was issued to retain consulting and integration firms to upgrade MTA's current end-of-life platform to Dimensions.

payload.event_name: UKG Workforce Central End of Life
payload.description: UKG Workforce Central reaches end of life and will no longer be licensed and supported.
Target date for MTA to go live with UKG Dimensions; effective date when UKG will only support Dimensions.
Contract with DiRAD was modified to add necessary services for enhanced self-service options, security, and to optimize contact center designs, in the amount of $914,945.

payload.event_name: Original CCaaS Contract Award to DiRAD
payload.description: MTA Board approved award to DiRAD to transform customer and employee call centers into cloud-based CCaaS platform using Genesys Cloud.
Accenture found responsible notwithstanding significant adverse information, approved by MTA Chief Administrative Officer in consultation with MTA General Counsel.

payload.claim_text: This is a time-sensitive project, as UKG has announced that its WFC has reached its end of life and will no longer be licensed and supported as of December 31, 2025.
payload.description: UKG Workforce Central end-of-life timeline drives urgency of Kronos Systems Integration project.

payload.claim_text: The Contract award allowed adding future call centers as needed where DiRAD would also provide annual license subscriptions, training, and recurring maintenance and technical support.
payload.description: Original CCaaS contract anticipated future expansion, enabling this Paratransit modification.

payload.claim_text: DiRAD has certified that pursuant to EO16 it is not doing business in Russia.
payload.description: EO16 certification for DiRAD Technologies.

payload.claim_text: Both IBM and Accenture have certified that pursuant to EO 16 they are not doing business in Russia.
payload.description: EO16 certification for IBM and Accenture.

payload.claim_text: DiRAD's price of $23,311,582 for this modification reflects an overall reduction of $391,565 in software licensing.
payload.description: Modification price reflects a reduction in software licensing costs.
Kronos Systems Integration project was preceded by an RFP issued on October 5, 2023.
UKG Workforce Central end-of-life on December 31, 2025 drives the timeline for Kronos migration project.
Target go-live for UKG Dimensions is January 1, 2026.
Kronos Systems Integration contracts are on the January 29, 2024 MTA Headquarters Committee agenda.
Kronos Systems Integration contracts are on the January 31, 2024 MTA Board agenda for approval.
IBM awarded a retainer contract for Kronos Systems Integration and Implementation consulting services.
Accenture LLP awarded a retainer contract for Kronos Systems Integration and Implementation consulting services.
Original CCaaS contract was awarded to DiRAD in March 2021, this modification expands that contract.
Prior modification to CCaaS contract on November 19, 2022 added enhanced self-service options and security.
DiRAD Technologies is the contractor implementing CCaaS for Paratransit.
CCaaS expansion will serve NYCT Paratransit Department's Contact Center.
CCaaS Paratransit modification is on the January 29, 2024 MTA Headquarters Committee agenda.
CCaaS Paratransit modification is on the January 31, 2024 MTA Board agenda for approval.
